Name: Deductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Understand the Goal The objective is to analyze a provided event log of a Customer Support Ticket Resolution process I need to identify slow cases outliers determine root causes for the delays patterns bottlenecks and provide recommendations

Name: Pattern Recognition - PE
Text: Analyze the Data I need to process the tabular data provided in the prompt Ill calculate the duration for each case Case ID from the first event Receive Ticket to the last event Close Ticket I also need to look at the time deltas between specific activities to find waiting times

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 101 Start 2024 03 01 08 00 End 2024 03 01 10 15 Total Duration 2 hours 15 minutes Flow Receive Triage 10m Assign 10m Investigate 40m wait Resolve 1h Close 15m Notes Straightforward no escalation

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 102 Start 2024 03 01 08 05 End 2024 03 02 09 15 Total Duration 25 hours 10 minutes Flow Receive Triage 25m Assign 30m Escalate 2h 30m Investigate 2h Wait Overnight Resolve Close Notes Escalation involved Significant gap between Investigate Issue 14 00 and Resolve Ticket Next Day 09 00

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 103 Start 2024 03 01 08 10 End 2024 03 01 09 30 Total Duration 1 hour 20 minutes Flow Receive Triage 5m Assign 15m Investigate 15m Resolve 30m Close 15m Notes Fastest case No escalation Efficient

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 104 Start 2024 03 01 08 20 End 2024 03 02 08 30 Total Duration 24 hours 10 minutes Flow Receive Triage 40m Assign 30m Wait 3h 30m Investigate Wait Overnight Resolve Close Notes No escalation marked but long wait between Assign and Investigate 09 30 to 13 00 Long wait between Investigate and Resolve 13 00 to Next Day 08 00

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Case 105 Start 2024 03 01 08 25 End 2024 03 03 09 30 Total Duration 49 hours 5 minutes Flow Receive Triage 10m Assign 25m Investigate 10m Escalate 50m Wait Long Investigate Wait Overnight Resolve Close Notes Longest case Escalation involved Massive gap between Escalate 10 00 Day 1 and next Investigate 14 00 Day 2

Name: Inductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Synthesize Findings Outliers Case 105 49 hrs Case 102 25 hrs Case 104 24 hrs Normal Case 101 2 25 hrs Case 103 1 33 hrs Pattern 1 Escalations Cases 102 and 105 have escalations Case 105 is the slowest Escalation seems correlated with higher duration Pattern 2 Overnight Day Boundaries Cases 102 104 and 105 all span multiple days Specifically there is a gap between afternoon activities and resolution the next morning This suggests work isnt happening outside business hours or theres a handover delay Pattern 3 Pre Investigation Wait Case 104 has a long wait between Assign and Investigate 3 5 hours Case 102 has a wait between Assign and Escalate 2 5 hours Pattern 4 Post Investigation Wait Cases 102 104 and 105 all have a long gap between the final Investigate and Resolve In 102 and 104 investigation ends late afternoon 14 00 and 13 00 resolution happens next morning In 105 investigation ends Day 2 afternoon resolution Day 3 morning

Name: Abductive Reasoning - PE
Text: Root Causes 1 Escalation Handover Latency When a ticket goes to Level 2 there is a significant delay before they pick it up for example Case 105 Escalated Day 1 10 00 Investigated Day 2 14 00 2 End of Day Handover Tickets investigated late in the day are not resolved until the next morning This implies a batching of resolutions or lack of capacity to close tickets same day if they get complex 3 Initial Assignment Lag Some agents take time to start investigating after assignment Case 104

Name: Heuristic Reasoning - PE
Text: Recommendations 1 Improve Level 2 SLA Service Level Agreement 2 Encourage same day resolution for tickets investigated before a certain cutoff time 3 Monitor Assign to Investigate time for Level 1 agents

Name: Backtracking - PE
Text: Self Correction on Case 104 Case 104 does not show an Escalate activity yet it takes 24 hours Why The gap is between Investigate Issue Day 1 13 00 and Resolve Ticket Day 2 08 00 This suggests the agent found the solution but didnt close it until the next day or the investigation was paused Wait looking closely at 104 Assign 09 30 Investigate 13 00 3 5h wait Investigate 13 00 Resolve 08 00 next day 19h wait This looks like an agent workload issue or a parked ticket Self Correction on Case 105 Escalate 10 00 Day 1 Investigate 14 00 Day 2 That is a 28 hour gap just waiting for Level 2 This is the biggest bottleneck

Name: Validation - PE
Text: Final Review Does this answer the prompt fully Identify long cases Yes 102 104 105 Determine root causes Yes Escalation delays End of Day boundaries Initial lag Explain factors recommendations Yes Calculation Check 101 08 00 to 10 15 2h 15m 102 08 05 Day 1 to 09 15 Day 2 25h 10m 103 08 10 to 09 30 1h 20m 104 08 20 Day 1 to 08 30 Day 2 24h 10m 105 08 25 Day 1 to 09 30 Day 3 49h 5m Average of fast cases 1h 47m Average of slow cases 32h Significant difference confirmed
